feature: Checkpoint artifact diff renderer per artifact type
description: Add per-artifact-type renderers that produce a concise human-readable summary for each checkpoint surface (research doc, plan spec, sprint contract, curator briefing, generator output diff, evaluator result, code-reviewer report, sprint summary, pipeline summary). Replaces the generic 'first 40 lines' renderer from Sprint 8 with structured per-type summaries.

successCriteria.description: src/orchestrator/checkpoints/renderers/ contains one renderer per artifact type (research.ts, plan.ts, sprint-contract.ts, curator-briefing.ts, generator-diff.ts, eval-result.ts, code-review.ts, sprint-summary.ts, pipeline-summary.ts). Each exports a render(artifact): string function.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: ls src/orchestrator/checkpoints/renderers/. Verify all 9 files exist. Read each to verify render() export.

successCriteria.description: Renderer registry maps artifactType → renderer; CheckpointMechanism implementations resolve the right renderer for each artifact. Falls back to generic text dump for unknown types with warning.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read registry. Verify mapping. Test: passing unknown type → generic fallback + warning.

successCriteria.description: Research doc renderer shows: title (from H1), assumptions count, files explored count, key findings count, first 3 lines of executive summary. Total output <500 lines (truncated with marker if source is larger).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read renderer. Render a sample research doc. Verify field extraction and line cap.

successCriteria.description: Plan spec renderer shows: title, ambiguity score, features count, sprints count, assumptions list, out-of-scope list. Total output <300 lines.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Render a sample plan spec. Verify fields and cap.

successCriteria.description: Sprint contract renderer shows: contractId, feature, expectedChanges paths, successCriteria count + first 5, dependencies. Total output <200 lines.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Render a sample contract. Verify fields and cap.

successCriteria.description: Generator-diff renderer shows: git diff --stat of the sprint's commits, list of files changed (created/modified/deleted), commit count. Truncates per-file diff at 50 lines per file with marker.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Render a fixture diff. Verify stat-only + truncation.

successCriteria.description: Eval-result renderer shows: overallResult, score (passed/failed/total), failing criteria with verification methods, strategy outputs (exit codes only, no full stdout). Total output <300 lines.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Render a sample eval result. Verify fields and cap.

successCriteria.description: Code-review renderer shows: summary, critical count + first 5 with evidence, important count, minor count, approved areas. Total output <300 lines.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Render a sample review. Verify fields and cap.

successCriteria.description: Sprint 8 (CLI) and Sprint 9 (disk) and Sprint 10 (PR) mechanisms now use these renderers instead of the generic first-40-lines logic. Existing tests for those mechanisms updated to assert the new render output.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Grep src/orchestrator/checkpoints/mechanisms/ for renderer usage. Verify removal of generic-truncate logic.

successCriteria.description: Unit tests cover each renderer with at least one happy-path sample. Run npm run test — assert exit 0.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Locate tests/orchestrator/checkpoints/renderers/. Verify one test file per renderer minimum.

expectedChanges.description: Use renderer registry for pending file summary
Use renderer registry for PR comment body

generatorNotes:
Renderers are pure functions: artifact in, string (markdown) out. No side effects, no file IO, no network — just parsing and formatting. This makes them trivial to test and reuse across mechanisms.

Artifact types are stable JSON shapes already in use elsewhere in agent-bober. Parse them from disk by reading the path in artifact.path, or accept inline artifact data when provided. Each renderer must handle both modes.

For the generator-diff renderer: shell out to `git diff --stat <base>..<head>` for the stat view; for per-file content, use `git diff --unified=3 <path>` truncated at 50 lines. Don't render binary file diffs (skip with marker).

Truncation marker convention (consistent across all renderers):
```
... <N more lines truncated, see <path>:<startLine> for full content>
```

Mechanism updates: each mechanism currently uses something like `prepareSummary(artifact)`. Replace with `import { render } from '../renderers/registry'; const summary = render(artifact);`. The registry routes to the right renderer by `artifact.type`. CLI prints to stderr; disk writes to pending JSON; PR includes in comment body.

For PR mechanism specifically: the rendered summary embedded as a fenced markdown code block in the PR comment. GitHub renders the markdown nicely, but very long content can hit GitHub's comment-size cap (65KB) — the 200-500 line per-renderer caps prevent this.
evaluatorNotes:
Verify all 9 renderers exist and each is exercised by a unit test. Verify the truncation marker is CONSISTENT across renderers (the format above). Verify each renderer's output is markdown (not plain text or JSON) — checkpoint mechanisms display markdown.

Verify generator-diff renderer SKIPS binary files (rendering binary as text breaks PR comments and CLI terminals). Test: include a binary file in the fixture, verify it's listed but not rendered inline.

Check for code duplication across renderers: if every renderer reimplements 'read frontmatter from markdown', factor to a shared util. But don't over-abstract — three renderers sharing a utility is fine; one renderer using its own logic is fine.

Verify Sprint 8/9/10 mechanism tests still pass after the renderer integration. If the old generic-truncate tests still exist and now assert on different output, they need updating (not deletion — update to match new format).

Line caps in success criteria are MAXIMUMS not targets. A 50-line render for a small artifact is correct. A 500-line render for a small artifact is over-rendering.
